outlook.headline: NAND recovery improves, though demand is still upgrade-led
outlook.summary: The next 1-2 quarters still point to an improving NAND backdrop: AI/data-center demand is hot, supply remains tight, and multiple vendors are seeing early recovery or upgrade-led spending. The nuance is that much of the near-term lift is still being met by upgrades rather than new wafer starts, and mention momentum has faded, so watch for whether this recovery broadens into stronger pricing and volume growth.

drivers.label: AI/data-center demand
drivers.detail: Hot demand from data centers and AI is lifting NAND bit demand and supporting higher inventory commitments.

drivers.label: Tight industry supply
drivers.detail: Cleanroom limits and committed supply keep the market tight, which supports pricing and utilization.

drivers.label: Early recovery / upgrade-led spending
drivers.detail: Several reads point to early recovery, but near-term demand is still being met mainly by upgrades rather than new wafer starts.

drivers.label: HDD substitution remains a drag
drivers.detail: A wider HDD-versus-NAND price gap keeps HDDs the cheaper storage choice, limiting some NAND demand conversion.

risks: Upgrades do not translate into new wafer starts or broader capacity expansion.
Supply tightness eases faster than expected, pressuring pricing and utilization.
HDD price advantage continues to cap NAND substitution demand.
